headline: Bills Prep for New Stadium Era as Camp Nears
writeup: The Buffalo Bills are generating serious buzz as training camp approaches, with two open practices scheduled at the brand-new Highmark Stadium on August 8 and August 18, giving fans their first real look at the team's new home. Off the field, the Bills are exploring mixed-use development options around the old Highmark Stadium site, signaling a major transformation for the Orchard Park area. On the coaching side, new defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard is drawing attention as analysts project what his scheme will look like, with pressure on him to fix the defense's late-down struggles that plagued the unit last season. Offensively, quarterback Josh Allen remains the centerpiece, with his offensive line earning top-five recognition league-wide and Allen himself making headlines for his career goals and offseason preparation. With Sean McDermott's era now in the rearview mirror, the Bills appear energized and poised for a fresh chapter under new leadership.
